accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurate context; the resolution was approved December 2, 2025.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the non-binding nature and lack of new statutory authority are correct characterizations.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose of requesting a one-time display on the National Mall and Washington Monument for the 250th anniversary.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The resolution makes no change to existing law, and the summary correctly notes this.

exceptions_conditions.why: Correctly notes the non-binding 'request' nature and the temporary, one-time, five-night limit.

effective_dates.why: Accurately states the December 31, 2025–January 5, 2026 display window and the December 2, 2025 enactment date.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures both the display request and the transmittal request to the Clerk.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and non-partisan throughout.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names the Secretary of the Interior, Clerk of the House, and Semiquincentennial Commission.

correct_figures.why: All dates and the five-night duration are stated correctly.
